summary: The most commonly used tooth numbering system in dental textbooks is the FDI system, which serves as the global standard for tooth notation and is frequently employed in academic and international contexts. This dominance stems from its widespread adoption in both research and curriculum design, making it prevalent in textbook material even without explicit documentation.
